behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at the module handoff boundary (history manager -> LLM request builder), not just pure list slicing. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; a fake LLM client capturing payload is sufficient. LLM-DEP: correctness does not depend on model judgment, only on what payload is sent. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is strictly about trimming before send, not model output quality or multi-step agent decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire conversation service to a spy/fake LLM client, provide >10 prior messages, invoke reply generation, and assert the outbound LLM payload contains exactly the latest 10 messages in correct order and roles.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness is about response relevance/quality from a real model; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide real failures. MOCK-HIDE: mocked content cannot validate topicality failures. MULTI-STEP: this behavior is a single generation-quality check, not a required multi-agent pipeline. Rejection gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior text does not require multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run prompts against the real production model with representative customer questions and score whether answers are on-topic and question-resolving (with rubric or human-labeled set), failing if generic/off-topic rate exceeds threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: identifying "too formal" depends on model judgment; stubs hide this. MULTI-STEP: behavior explicitly couples tone judgment and downstream regeneration trigger, requiring sequential model-involved stages (initial response context -> tone check decision -> regenerate). INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d): orchestration across LLM-involved handoff should not stay Integration when model decision governs routing. Rejection gate: Agentic-only is rejected because behavior text includes both identification and regeneration trigger, i.e., a chained sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real model calls for casual-tier scenarios likely to elicit formal drafts; verify at least one draft is flagged as too formal and that regeneration is invoked, producing a replacement candidate recorded as regenerated.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in service->repository state handoff and transaction/persistence mapping across modules. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local/in-process test DB; no real external network or OS primitive required. LLM-DEP: model output quality is irrelevant; only captured artifacts and timestamps matter. MULTI-STEP: no multi-agentic correctness requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local test database, run one interaction including forced regeneration, then assert stored records contain user message, first draft, regeneration(s), final response linkage, and monotonic/expected timestamps.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state handoff between tone-check result, regeneration path, and response presenter. LLM-DEP: behavior text conditions on "when tone check triggers"; correctness target is replacement/visibility logic, which is testable with deterministic stubs once trigger=true. REAL-INFRA: no real network/OS/subprocess required. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by the behavior text focusing on post-trigger replacement semantics, not whether model decisions are correct.
behaviors.test_description: With tone-check forced to trigger and deterministic first/second drafts, run request through orchestration and assert returned customer-visible response equals second draft, while first draft is marked rejected/internal only.
